problem.summary: Transpose a row-major matrix. The baseline reads coalesced but writes with a stride. The optimized version stages tiles in shared memory and pads the shared tile to reduce bank conflicts.
outputs: output[col * rows + row] equals input[row * cols + col]
constraints: rows and cols may not be multiples of tile dimensions
input and output do not alias

verification.method: Compare output against CPU transpose for square and rectangular matrices. The 4096x4096 and 1009x2039 gfx1201 HIP runs passed with max_abs_error=0 and bad_count=0.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. On 2026-05-28, AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201 HIP-event timing measured 14.53330 ms naive transpose versus 0.65830 ms shared-memory tiled transpose for 4096x4096 fp32, speedup 22.077017x. A non-square/tail 1009x2039 sweep measured 0.05185 ms baseline versus 0.00670 ms optimized, speedup 7.738806x. No rocprofiler/rocprof counters were collected, so bank-conflict and memory-transaction explanations remain expected signals only.
